CONTENT.Club career: Drommel used to play as a midfielder, but turned goalkeeper before joining the Almere City youth set-up. He then had a season with IJsselmeervogels, before joining FC Twente in 2014. He made his Eredivisie debut on 12 September 2015 against AFC Ajax in a 2–2 draw.In the 2019–20 and 2020–21 seasons, he appeared in all but one KNVB Cup match. In March 2021, Twente triggered a one-year option in contract. Shortly afterwards, Drommel was sold to PSV for €3.5 million, signing a five-year contract effective from the 2021–22 season.
CONTENT.Personal life: His father Piet-Jan Drommel played for AZ and SC Telstar.
CONTENT.External links: Profile at the PSV Eindhoven websiteNetherlands profile at Ons Oranje
CONTENT.International career: Drommel was called up to the senior Netherlands squad in November 2020.

SUMMARY: Joël Drommel (born 16 November 1996) is a Dutch professional footballer who plays as a goalkeeper for Eredivisie side PSV.
